121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209

211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234

236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)
335	
336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace
349	
350	
351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits
396	
397	
398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

523	# torch.multinomial forces a GPU<->CPU sync.
524	# Therefore, we use an optimized implementation instead.
525	# Note that we always sample with replacement.
526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)
548

550	def _top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer(
551	        probs: torch.Tensor, top_ks: torch.Tensor, top_ps: torch.Tensor,
552	        num_samples: int, seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]]):
553	    if num_samples > 1:
554	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
555	        top_ks = top_ks.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
556	        top_ps = top_ps.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
557	    batch_next_token_ids = flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling(
558	        probs,
559	        top_ks,
560	        top_ps,
561	    )
562	    return batch_next_token_ids.view(-1, num_samples)

565	def get_pythonized_sample_results(
566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''
582	
583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue
600	        (seq_group_id, seq_groups) = sample_metadata[sampling_type]
601	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
602	            sample_results = _greedy_sample(seq_groups, greedy_samples)
603	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
604	            sample_results = _random_sample(seq_groups,
605	                                            multinomial_samples[sampling_type])
606	        sample_results_dict.update(zip(seq_group_id, sample_results))
607	
608	    return [
609	        sample_results_dict.get(i, ([], []))
610	        for i in range(len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups))
611	    ]

614	def _sample_with_torch(
615	    probs: torch.Tensor,
616	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
617	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
618	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
619	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
620	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
621	) -> SampleReturnType:
622	    '''Torch-oriented _sample() implementation.
623	
624	    Single-step scheduling:
625	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
626	    * Immediately Pythonize sampling result
627	
628	    Multi-step scheduling:
629	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
630	    * Defer Pythonization & preserve GPU-side
631	      tensors required for Pythonization
632	    '''
633	
634	    categorized_seq_group_ids: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
635	        t: []
636	        for t in SamplingType
637	    }
638	    categorized_sample_indices = sampling_metadata.categorized_sample_indices
639	    for i, seq_group in enumerate(sampling_metadata.seq_groups):
640	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
641	        sampling_type = sampling_params.sampling_type
642	        categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type].append(i)
643	
644	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType = {}
645	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType = {}
646	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType = {}
647	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
648	
649	    # Create output tensor for sampled token ids.
650	    if include_gpu_probs_tensor:
651	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = torch.full((logprobs.shape[0], 1),
652	                                              VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
653	                                              dtype=torch.long,
654	                                              device=logprobs.device)
655	    else:
656	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = None
657	
658	    # Counterintiutively, having two loops here is actually faster.
659	    # The first loop can run without waiting on GPU<->CPU sync.
660	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
661	        sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices[sampling_type]
662	        num_tokens = len(sample_indices)
663	        if num_tokens == 0:
664	            continue
665	
666	        seq_group_id = categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type]
667	        seq_groups = [sampling_metadata.seq_groups[i] for i in seq_group_id]
668	        sample_metadata[sampling_type] = (seq_group_id, seq_groups)
669	        long_sample_indices = sample_indices.long()
670	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
671	            greedy_samples = torch.argmax(logprobs[long_sample_indices],
672	                                          dim=-1)
673	
674	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
675	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.

677	                    long_sample_indices] = greedy_samples.unsqueeze(-1)

679	            if modify_greedy_probs:

684	                                             long_sample_indices,

687	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
688	            max_n_in_batch = 1
689	            for seq_group in seq_groups:
690	                if seq_group.is_prompt:
691	                    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
692	                    max_n_in_batch = max(max_n_in_batch, sampling_params.n)
693	            seq_groups_arg = (None if sampling_type == SamplingType.RANDOM else
694	                              seq_groups)

696	            if flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is not None:
697	                logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
698	                               "per-request generators. Falling back to "
699	                               "PyTorch-native implementation.")

3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
14	
15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
16	
17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """

19	@dataclass
20	class SequenceGroupToSample:
21	    # |---------- N-1 iteration --------|
22	    # |---------------- N iteration ---------------------|
23	    # |- tokenA -|......................|-- newTokens ---|
24	    # |---------- context_len ----------|
25	    # |-------------------- seq_len ----------------------|
26	    #                                   |-- query_len ---|
27	
28	    # Sequence ids for the sequence group in a previous step.
29	    seq_ids: list[int]
30	    sampling_params: SamplingParams
31	    # seq_id -> sequence data.
32	    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
33	    # The length of the sequence (all tokens seen in the past + new token to
34	    # compute attention) of the sequence group. None if it is in a decode
35	    # stage.
36	    seq_len: Optional[int]
37	    # The length of new query tokens to compute in the current step. None if it
38	    # is in a decode stage. The length of query_len <= seq_len if chunked
39	    # prefill is enabled.
40	    query_len: Optional[int]
41	    # A random number generator for sampling.
42	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
43	    # True if the sequence group is in prefill stage. False if it is in a
44	    # decode stage.
45	    is_prompt: bool
46	    # Query token indices from logits. to compute prompt logprob. Empty if
47	    # prompt logprob is not required.
48	    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
49	    # Sample token indices from logits. Empty if sampling is not required.
50	    sample_indices: list[int]
51	
52	    @property
53	    def do_sample(self):
54	        return len(self.sample_indices) > 0
55	
56	    def __post_init__(self):
57	        if len(self.prompt_logprob_indices) > 0:
58	            assert self.sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None
59	        if self.is_prompt:
60	            assert self.seq_len is not None
61	            assert self.query_len is not None
62	
63	
64	def gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs: int):
65	    return lambda: SequenceGroupToSample(
66	        seq_ids=[0] * num_seqs,
67	        sampling_params=None,
68	        seq_data=None,  # type: ignore
69	        seq_len=0,
70	        query_len=0,
71	        generator=None,
72	        is_prompt=True,
73	        prompt_logprob_indices=[],
74	        sample_indices=[],
75	    )

78	class SamplingMetadataCache:
79	    """Used to cache SamplingMetadata objects between scheduler iterations"""
80	
81	    def __init__(self):
82	        self._seq_group_to_sample_cache: dict[int, PyObjectCache] = {}
83	
84	    def get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(self, num_seqs):
85	        if num_seqs not in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache:
86	            self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs] = PyObjectCache(
87	                gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs))
88	
89	        obj = self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs].get_object()
90	        return obj
91	
92	    def reset(self):
93	        for cache in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache.values():
94	            cache.reset()

97	class SamplingMetadata:
98	    """Metadata for input sequences. Used in sampler.
99	
100	    The usage is as follow;
101	    ```
102	    hidden_states = execute_model(...)
103	    logits = hidden_states[sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices]
104	    sample(logits)
105	
106	    def sample(logits):
107	        # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling....
108	    ```
109	
110	    Args:
111	        seq_groups: List of batched sequence groups.
112	        selected_token_indices: (num_query_tokens_to_logprob). Indices to find
113	            logits from the initial model output hidden states.
114	        categorized_sample_indices: SamplingType -> token indices to sample.
115	            Each token indices is 2D tensor of (num_indices, num_indices) where
116	            the first item means the sample index within the returned logit
117	            (before pruning padding), and the second item means the sample
118	            index after pruning using selected_token_indices.
119	            For example, if the returned logit is [1, 2, 3], and we select
120	            [1, 2] for sampling, the pruned logit will be [2, 3]. In this case,
121	            The first tuple is [1, 2] (sampled index within original logit),
122	            and the second tuple is [0, 1] (sampled index within pruned logit).
123	        num_prompts: Number of prompt sequence groups in seq_groups.
124	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: Indicates if we want to skip the GPU=>CPU
125	            serialization of token outputs.
126	        reuse_sampling_tensors: Indicates if we want to reuse sampling
127	            tensors that are part of the sampler forward pass. Currently,
128	            it is mainly used for multi-step decode.
129	
130	    """
131	
132	    def __init__(
133	        self,
134	        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
135	        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
136	        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
137	        num_prompts: int,
138	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
139	        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
140	    ) -> None:
141	        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
142	        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
143	        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
144	        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
145	        self.skip_sampler_cpu_output = skip_sampler_cpu_output
146	        self.reuse_sampling_tensors = reuse_sampling_tensors
147	
148	    @staticmethod
149	    def prepare(
150	        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
151	        seq_lens: list[int],
152	        query_lens: list[int],
153	        device: str,
154	        pin_memory: bool,
155	        generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
156	        cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
157	    ) -> "SamplingMetadata":
158	        (
159	            seq_groups,
160	            selected_token_indices,
161	            categorized_sample_indices,
162	            num_prompts,
163	        ) = _prepare_seq_groups(seq_group_metadata_list, seq_lens, query_lens,
164	                                device, generators, cache)
165	        selected_token_indices = async_tensor_h2d(
166	            selected_token_indices,
167	            dtype=torch.long,
168	            target_device=device,
169	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
170	        )
171	        categorized_sample_indices = {
172	            t:
173	            async_tensor_h2d(
174	                seq_ids,
175	                dtype=torch.int,
176	                target_device=device,
177	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
178	            )
179	            for t, seq_ids in categorized_sample_indices.items()
180	        }
181	
182	        sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata(
183	            seq_groups=seq_groups,
184	            selected_token_indices=selected_token_indices,
185	            categorized_sample_indices=categorized_sample_indices,
186	            num_prompts=num_prompts,
187	        )
188	        return sampling_metadata
189	
190	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
191	        return (
192	            "SamplingMetadata("
193	            f"seq_groups={self.seq_groups}, "
194	            f"selected_token_indices={self.selected_token_indices}, "
195	            f"categorized_sample_indices={self.categorized_sample_indices})")
196	
197	
198	def _prepare_seq_groups(
199	    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
200	    seq_lens: list[int],
201	    query_lens: list[int],
202	    device: str,
203	    generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
204	    cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
205	) -> tuple[
206	        list[SequenceGroupToSample],
207	        list[int],
208	        dict[SamplingType, list[int]],
209	        int,
210	]:
211	    """Prepare sequence groups and indices for sampling.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        seq_group_metadata_list: A list of sequence group to batch.
215	        seq_lens: A list of sequence lens per sequence group.
216	            Index of prompt len should match with seq_group_metadata_list.
217	        query_lens: A list of query lengths. Prompt lens include the length
218	            of entire prompt tokens, and it could be shorter.
219	        device: A device to use for random number generators,
220	            `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`.
221	        generators: A store of per-request random number generators used
222	            for seeded requests.
223	
224	    Returns:
225	        seq_groups: A list of sequence group to sample.
226	        selected_token_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
227	        categorized_sample_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
228	        num_prompts: Total number of prompts from `seq_group_metadata_list`.
229	    """
230	    # Batched sequence groups for the current model forward stsep.
231	    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample] = []
232	    # A list of token indices to sample/compute logprob. It is used to
233	    # prune the outcome logits from the model for the performance.
234	    selected_token_indices: list[int] = []
235	    # Used for selected_token_indices.
236	    model_output_idx = 0
237	
238	    # Sampling type -> (
239	    # indices to sample/prompt logprob within pruned output logits,
240	    # indices to sample within pruned logits)
241	    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
242	        t: []
243	        for t in SamplingType
244	    }
245	    # Index of logits to compute logprob. Logits include both prompt logprob
246	    # and sample logprob indices.
247	    logit_idx = 0
248	    # Total number of prompts from given sequence groups.
249	    num_prompts = 0
250	
251	    for i, seq_group_metadata in enumerate(seq_group_metadata_list):
252	        seq_ids = seq_group_metadata.seq_data.keys()
253	
254	        if cache is not None:
255	            sample_obj = cache.get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(len(seq_ids))
256	
257	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
258	                sample_obj.seq_ids[j] = seq_id
259	
260	            sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices.clear()
261	            sample_obj.sample_indices.clear()
262	
263	        sampling_params = seq_group_metadata.sampling_params
264	        is_prompt = seq_group_metadata.is_prompt
265	        generator: Optional[torch.Generator] = None
266	        # If the current seq group is in decode stage, it is None.
267	        seq_len: Optional[int] = None
268	        query_len: Optional[int] = None
269	        prompt_logprob_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices
270	                                             if cache is not None else [])
271	        sample_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.sample_indices
272	                                     if cache is not None else [])
273	        do_sample = seq_group_metadata.do_sample
274	
275	        if seq_group_metadata.is_prompt:
276	            if sampling_params.seed is not None:
277	                generator = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(
278	                    sampling_params.seed)
279	                if generators is not None:
280	                    generators[seq_group_metadata.request_id] = generator
281	
282	            num_prompts += 1
283	            num_prefill_sample = len(seq_ids)
284	            assert num_prefill_sample == 1
285	            assert query_lens is not None and seq_lens is not None
286	            query_len, seq_len = query_lens[i], seq_lens[i]
287	            # If we need sampling, exclude num_prefill_sample tokens from
288	            # prompt logprob.
289	            prompt_logprob_len = (query_len - num_prefill_sample
290	                                  if do_sample else query_len)
291	            sample_len = num_prefill_sample if do_sample else 0
292	        else:
293	            # Decode
294	            prompt_logprob_len = 0
295	            query_len = query_lens[i] if query_lens is not None and len(
296	                query_lens) > 0 else 1
297	            sample_len = len(seq_ids) * query_len if do_sample else 0
298	
299	            if sampling_params.seed is not None and generators is not None:
300	                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)

300	                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)
301	
302	        # Update indices to select from the model output.
303	        """
304	        This blocks computes selected_token_indices which is used in the
305	        following way.
306	
307	        hidden_states = model(...)
308	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
309	        """
310	
311	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
312	            selected_token_indices.extend(
313	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
314	        model_output_idx += prompt_logprob_len
315	        if do_sample:
316	            selected_token_indices.extend(
317	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + sample_len))
318	        model_output_idx += sample_len
319	
320	        # We now find indices for logprob computation and sampling.
321	        """
322	        This block computes categorized_sample_indices which is used in the
323	        following way.
324	
325	        hidden_states = model(...)
326	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
327	        def sample(logits):
328	           # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling.
329	           # prompt_logprob_indices to find prompt logprob indices.
330	           # sample_indices to find sample indices.
331	        """
332	
333	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
334	            prompt_logprob_indices.extend(
335	                range(logit_idx, logit_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
336	            logit_idx += prompt_logprob_len
337	        if do_sample:
338	            sample_indices.extend(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len))
339	            categorized_sample_indices[sampling_params.sampling_type].extend(
340	                list(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len)))
341	            logit_idx += sample_len
342	
343	        if cache is not None:
344	            sample_obj.sampling_params = sampling_params
345	            sample_obj.seq_data = seq_group_metadata.seq_data
346	            sample_obj.seq_len = seq_len
347	            sample_obj.query_len = query_len
348	            sample_obj.generator = generator
349	            sample_obj.is_prompt = is_prompt
350	        else:
351	            sample_obj = SequenceGroupToSample(
352	                seq_ids=list(seq_ids),
353	                sampling_params=sampling_params,
354	                seq_data=seq_group_metadata.seq_data,
355	                seq_len=seq_len,
356	                query_len=query_len,
357	                generator=generator,
358	                is_prompt=is_prompt,
359	                prompt_logprob_indices=list(prompt_logprob_indices),
360	                sample_indices=list(sample_indices),
361	            )
362	
363	        seq_groups.append(sample_obj)
364	
365	    if cache is not None:
366	        cache.reset()
367	
368	    return (seq_groups, selected_token_indices, categorized_sample_indices,
369	            num_prompts)

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False

447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len

454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt

489	            prompt_tokens,

503	        min_ps: list[float],
504	        presence_penalties: list[float],

519	        if do_penalties:

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

18	# Maximum number of speculative draft tokens allowed per request in a single

23	class RejectionSampler(nn.Module):
24	    """
25	    The implementation strictly follows the algorithm described in
26	        https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.17192.
27	    However, we want to clarify the terminology used in the implementation:
28	    accepted tokens: tokens that are accepted based on the relationship
29	            between the "raw" draft and target probabilities.
30	    recovered tokens: tokens that are sampled based on the adjusted probability
31	        distribution, which is derived from both the draft and target
32	        probabilities.
33	    bonus tokens:
34	        If all proposed tokens are accepted, the bonus token is added to the
35	        end of the sequence. The bonus token is only sampled from the target
36	        probabilities. We pass in the bonus tokens instead of sampling them
37	        in the rejection sampler to allow for more flexibility in the
38	        sampling process. For example, we can use top_p, top_k sampling for
39	        bonus tokens, while spec decode does not support these sampling
40	        strategies.
41	    output tokens:
42	        Tokens are finally generated with the rejection sampler.
43	        output tokens = accepted tokens + recovered tokens + bonus tokens
44	    """
45	
46	    def forward(
47	        self,
48	        metadata: SpecDecodeMetadata,
49	        # [num_tokens, vocab_size]
50	        draft_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
51	        # [num_tokens, vocab_size]
52	        target_logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        # [batch_size, 1]
54	        bonus_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
55	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
56	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
57	        '''
58	        Args:
59	            metadata:
60	                Metadata for spec decoding.
61	            draft_probs (Optional[torch.Tensor]):
62	                Probability distribution for the draft tokens. Shape is
63	                [num_tokens, vocab_size]. Can be None if probabilities are
64	                not provided, which is the case for ngram spec decode.
65	            target_logits (torch.Tensor):
66	                Target model's logits probability distribution.
67	                Shape is [num_tokens, vocab_size]. Here, probabilities from
68	                different requests are flattened into a single tensor because
69	                this is the shape of the output logits.
70	                NOTE: `target_logits` can be updated in place to save memory.
71	            bonus_token_ids_tensor (torch.Tensor):
72	                A tensor containing bonus tokens. Shape is [batch_size, 1].
73	                Bonus tokens are added to the end of the sequence if all
74	                proposed tokens are accepted. We generate the bonus tokens
75	                outside of the rejection sampler with the default sampling
76	                strategy. It allows for more flexibility in the sampling
77	                process such as top_p, top_k sampling.
78	            sampling_metadata (vllm.v1.sample.metadata.SamplingMetadata):
79	                Additional metadata needed for sampling, such as temperature,
80	                top-k/top-p parameters, or other relevant information.
81	        Returns:
82	            output_token_ids (torch.Tensor):
83	                A tensor containing the final output token IDs.
84	        '''
85	        assert metadata.max_spec_len <= MAX_SPEC_LEN
86	        # [num_tokens, vocab_size]
87	        # NOTE(woosuk): `target_logits` can be updated in place inside the
88	        # `compute_probs` function.
89	        target_probs = compute_probs(
90	            target_logits,
91	            metadata.cu_num_draft_tokens,
92	            sampling_metadata,
93	        )
94	
95	        output_token_ids = rejection_sample(
96	            metadata.draft_token_ids,
97	            metadata.num_draft_tokens,
98	            metadata.max_spec_len,
99	            metadata.cu_num_draft_tokens,
100	            draft_probs,
101	            target_probs,
102	            bonus_token_ids,
103	            sampling_metadata,
104	        )
105	        return output_token_ids
106	
107	    @staticmethod
108	    def parse_output(
109	        output_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
110	        vocab_size: int,
111	    ) -> list[list[int]]:
112	        """Parse the output of the rejection sampler.
113	
114	        Args:
115	            output_token_ids: The sampled token IDs in shape
116	                [batch_size, max_spec_len + 1]. The rejected tokens are
117	                replaced with `PLACEHOLDER_TOKEN_ID` by the rejection sampler
118	                and will be filtered out in this function.
119	            vocab_size: The size of the vocabulary.
120	
121	        Returns:
122	            A list of lists of token IDs.
123	        """
124	        output_token_ids_np = output_token_ids.cpu().numpy()
125	        # Create mask for valid tokens.
126	        valid_mask = ((output_token_ids_np != PLACEHOLDER_TOKEN_ID) &
127	                      (output_token_ids_np < vocab_size))
128	        outputs = [
129	            row[valid_mask[i]].tolist()
130	            for i, row in enumerate(output_token_ids_np)
131	        ]
132	        return outputs
133

## Comprehensive Report: Sampling Parameters (top_p and top_k) Implementation in vLLM

I've completed a thorough exploration of how `top_p` and `top_k` sampling parameters are implemented throughout the vLLM codebase. Here are my findings:

### 1. **SamplingParams Class Definition**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Class Attributes (Lines 212-213):**
```python
top_p: float = 1.0
top_k: int = 0
```

**Default Values:**
- `top_p`: Default is `1.0` (considers all tokens)
- `top_k`: Default is `0` (considers all tokens; -1 is also accepted as disabled)

**Docstring Documentation (Lines 150-153):**
```python
top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
    to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
    to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
```

**Validation Logic (Lines 417-425):**
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")
if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```

**Post-Initialization Logic (Lines 377-382):**
When temperature is set to near-zero (greedy sampling), top_p and top_k are reset:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

**from_optional() Factory Method (Lines 252-325):**
Provides defaults when values are None:
```python
top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
top_k=top_k,
```

---

### 2. **SamplingTensors Data Structure**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Class Definition (Lines 372-397):**
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor       # Converted to tensor for batch processing
    top_ks: torch.Tensor       # Converted to tensor for batch processing
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Tensor Conversion Logic (Lines 415-445):**
- `top_p` and `top_k` from SamplingParams are collected into lists and converted to PyTorch tensors
- They are created with specific dtypes for GPU efficiency:
  - `top_ps`: float dtype (line 545-549)
  - `top_ks`: int dtype (lines 575-580)
- Memory is pinned for non-blocking GPU transfer
- Tensors are transferred to target device with `non_blocking=True`

**Key Collection Logic (Lines 415-420):**
```python
top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
```

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, p, k):
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Apply top-k: mask out tokens below k-th largest
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Apply top-p: mask based on cumulative probability
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-scatter back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

## VLLM Sampling Logic: Top_P and Top_K Filtering

### Main Sampler Files

#### 1. **V0 (Legacy) Sampler**
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 169-425)

- **Top-K/Top-P Operations File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` (lines 22-297)

#### 3. **CUDA Kernels**
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/csrc/sampler.cu` (repetition penalties only)

#### 4. **Sampling Parameters Definition**
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 118-250)

---

### Sampling Parameters Construction

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 372-597)

The `SamplingTensors` class converts `SamplingParams` fields into GPU tensors:

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor        # Shape: [batch_size], values in (0, 1]
    top_ks: torch.Tensor        # Shape: [batch_size], dtype=int32
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Key Construction Logic** (lines 386-495):
- Extracts `top_p` and `top_k` from each `SamplingParams` in the batch
- Clamps `top_k` to vocab_size: `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)`
- Handles special case: `top_k < 1` → set to `vocab_size` (disabled)
- Creates tensors with pinned memory for efficient GPU transfer
- Tensors transferred to GPU with `non_blocking=True`

**Parameter Defaults** (sampling_params.py, lines 205-214):
```python
top_p: float = 1.0          # Consider all tokens
top_k: int = 0              # Disabled (0 or -1 means all tokens)
min_p: float = 0.0
temperature: float = 1.0
```

---

### V0 Sampler Flow

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 225-334)

```
logits (raw)
    ↓
apply_allowed_token_ids (mask certain tokens)
    ↓
apply_penalties (presence/frequency/repetition)
    ↓
temperature scaling (logits /= temperature)
    ↓
_apply_top_k_top_p() [if not using FlashInfer]
    ↓
compute softmax → probs
    ↓
multinomial sampling
```

**Order of Operations**: 
1. **Top-K FIRST**, then **Top-P SECOND** (sequential, not fused)
2. Temperature applied **before** top-k/top-p

**Top-K/Top-P Implementation** (lines 398-424):

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,           # [batch_size]
    k: torch.Tensor,           # [batch_size]
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Step 1: Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Step 2: APPLY TOP-K
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # vocab_idx to keep
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask                # Create mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))   # Set below-top-k to -inf
    
    # Step 3: APPLY TOP-P on already top-k filtered logits
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)      # Cumsum cutoff
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False                             # Always keep at least 1 token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 4: Re-scatter back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**FlashInfer Integration** (V0):
- Conditional import at top of file (line 26-33)
- Only used if `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=True`
- Calls `flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling(probs, top_ks, top_ps)` (line 557)
- Default in V0: FlashInfer is disabled (`VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` defaults to False)

---

### V1 Sampler Flow

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (lines 25-77, 90-137)

```
logits (raw, float32)
    ↓
apply_allowed_token_ids
    ↓
apply_bad_words
    ↓
apply logits processors (non-argmax invariant)
    ↓
apply_penalties (presence/frequency/repetition)
    ↓
temperature scaling
    ↓
apply logits processors (argmax invariant)
    ↓
TopKTopPSampler.forward()  [handles top_k/top_p]
    ↓
```

### Core Filtering Functions

#### PyTorch Native Implementation

**Location**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331291380-7kkt5y/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` (lines 168-209)

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],   # [batch_size] or None
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],   # [batch_size] or None
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    ORDER: Always top-k BEFORE top-p (if both present)
    """
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        # Top-k only (optimized path, no full sort needed)
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    # Both or just top-p: sort entire vocab
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # APPLY TOP-K
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # [batch]
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # APPLY TOP-P
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Always keep at least one
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-scatter to original order
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Optimized Top-K Only Path** (lines 211-233):

```python
def apply_top_k_only(logits: torch.Tensor, k: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Optimized when top-p is disabled. Avoids full sorting of vocab.
    Uses torch.topk() instead of torch.sort()
    """
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    # Get top-k cutoff value
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

#### TPU-Optimized Implementation

**Location**: Lines 121-165 (same file)

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    TPU optimization: Avoids torch.scatter (extremely slow on TPU)
    Uses cumsum-based approach on sorted probs
    
    Algorithm: Find "cut-off" element that separates in/out of top-p
    """
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # TOP-K: Find the k-th largest probability
        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
        # No-op for disabled top-k
        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
        # Mask out below-top-k
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # TOP-P: Find cumsum threshold
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # At least one token
        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))

    return logits
```

#### FlashInfer Integration

**Location**: Lines 260-296

```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Wrapper around FlashInfer sampling functions
    Uses rejection sampling internally (faster, avoids full sorting)
    
    NOTE: Outputs NOT identical to random_sample due to different algorithm
    but statistically equivalent
    """
    assert not (k is None and p is None)
    
    if k is None:
        # Top-p only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        # Top-k only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        # Both top-k and top-p (fused kernel)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)

    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```
